info.name: Approach3-AdaptiveEpochMonitoring-MultiMetric-100K-iter5
info.description:
APPROACH 3 iter5: ADAPTIVE EPOCH MONITORING WITH CONFIGURABLE CADENCE
═══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

WHAT'S NEW IN iter5 vs iter4:
  iter4: 7 pre-planned phases (narrative lifecycle), fixed cadence
  iter5: ADAPTIVE cadence — monitoring frequency is a FUNCTION of risk state

KEY ARCHITECTURAL CHANGES:
  1. NO PHASES — single looping request via postman.setNextRequest()
     Each epoch = identical computation against current reserve state
     The cadence EMERGES from the metrics, nothing pre-planned

  2. ADAPTIVE FREQUENCY (both directions):
     NORMAL  → check every 24h (daily, all metrics green)
     WATCH   → check every 6h  (peg wobble, mild stress)
     ELEVATED→ check every 3h  (EW signals, near-threshold)
     CRITICAL→ check every 1h  (crisis, breach imminent/active)
     Escalation: immediate on threshold proximity
     De-escalation: requires N sustained good epochs (hysteresis)

  3. CONFIGURABLE BOUNDS (collection variables with defaults):
     All thresholds, intervals, caps, and hysteresis are configurable
     MAX_EPOCHS=30, SIM_END_HOURS=240, cadence intervals, etc.

  4. PORTFOLIO EVOLUTION:
     Contracts have realistic historical origination dates
     T-bill maturity detected dynamically (simTime > maturityDate)
     Cash depletes hourly from RedemptionPressureModel output
     New T-bill purchase triggered on Day 8 from fee income

  5. EPOCH TREND MODEL (NEW behavioral model #11):
     Tracks velocity of metric degradation across epochs
     3 consecutive declining epochs → TREND_DEGRADING signal
     De-escalation blocked while trend is degrading

  6. CADENCE ESCALATION IS ITSELF A RISK SIGNAL:
     The act of escalating from NORMAL→WATCH is logged
     Multiple escalations in short window → additional risk factor

BEHAVIORAL MODELS (11 — iter4's 10 + EpochTrendModel):
   1. BackingRatioModel        — MtM-adjusted reserves/supply
   2. RedemptionPressureModel   — 609-event calibrated curves
   3. MaturityLadderModel       — roll/keep/liquidate decision
   4. AssetQualityModel         — HQLA degradation
   5. ConcentrationDriftModel   — HHI across types/custodians
   6. ComplianceDriftModel      — STABLE/GENIUS/MiCA continuous
   7. EarlyWarningModel         — 4-signal composite posture
   8. ContinuousAttestationModel — ZK-ready data per epoch
   9. TwoDimensionalPrepayment  — T-bill early liquidation surface
  10. TwoDimensionalDepositTrx  — Mint gate acceptance surface
  11. EpochTrendModel (NEW)     — metric velocity + cadence signal

PORTFOLIO ($100,000 stablecoin reserves — 7 ACTUS PAM contracts):
  cash-reserve-001:    $10,000  (originated 2026-02-01, rolls daily)
  operating-cash-001:  $10,000  (originated 2026-02-01, rolls daily)
  tbill-4wk-001:       $18,000  (originated 2026-02-10, matures 2026-03-10)
  tbill-13wk-001:      $28,000  (originated 2026-01-20, matures 2026-04-21)
  tbill-26wk-001:      $22,000  (originated 2025-12-15, matures 2026-06-15)
  commpaper-90d-001:   $8,000   (originated 2026-01-15, matures 2026-04-15)
  defi-yield-001:      $4,000   (originated 2026-02-01, matures 2026-06-01)

PREREQUISITES:
  - actus-server on localhost:8083
  - risksrv3 on localhost:8082 (for Setup)
  - Open Postman Console (Ctrl+Alt+C) before running

Based on ACTUS official documentation:
  https://www.actusfrf.org/taxonomy
  https://www.actusfrf.org/dictionary

variable.description: Maximum epochs before forced termination (safety cap)
Minimum asset quality score for MINT_ALLOWED
EW signals count that triggers HALT
Peg deviation to escalate to WATCH (0.1%)
Peg deviation to escalate to ELEVATED (0.3%)
Peg deviation to escalate to CRITICAL (1.0%)
Consecutive good epochs needed to de-escalate one level
Consecutive declining epochs to trigger TREND_DEGRADING

item.name: Setup 4: TwoDimensionalPrepaymentModel — T-Bill Early Liquidation Surface
Setup 6: Register Scenario — Group all risk factors

exec: // Sets all configurable parameters, risk factor curves,
// initial portfolio, and epoch tracking variables
// ── Read configurable bounds (collection vars with defaults) ──
// Store config in environment for epoch evaluator to read
// ── Risk Factor Curves (hourly resolution over 10 days) ──
// Stored as JSON arrays: [{h: hourOffset, v: value}, ...]
// Epoch evaluator interpolates at current sim hour
description:'FDIC-insured bank deposits ($10,000) — HQLA L1, originated 2026-02-01'},
description:'Operating cash buffer ($10,000) — HQLA L1, originated 2026-02-01'},
description:'4-week US T-Bill ($18,000) — HQLA L1, originated 2026-02-10, matures 2026-03-10 (7 days)'},
description:'13-week US T-Bill ($28,000) — HQLA L1, originated 2026-01-20, matures 2026-04-21 (49 days)'},
description:'26-week US T-Bill ($22,000) — HQLA L1, originated 2025-12-15, matures 2026-06-15 (104 days)'},
description:'90-day A1/P1 Commercial Paper ($8,000) — HQLA L2A, originated 2026-01-15'},
description:'DeFi yield position ($4,000) — HQLA L2B, smart contract risk'}
console.log('  APPROACH 3 iter5: ADAPTIVE EPOCH MONITORING — INITIALIZED');
console.log('    Cadence:          NORMAL=' + NORMAL_HRS + 'h WATCH=' + WATCH_HRS + 'h ELEV=' + ELEVATED_HRS + 'h CRIT=' + CRITICAL_HRS + 'h');
console.log('    Thresholds:       Backing≥' + (BACKING_THRESHOLD*100) + '% Liq≥' + (LIQUIDITY_THRESHOLD*100) + '% Qual≥' + QUALITY_THRESHOLD);
console.log('    Peg escalation:   WATCH>' + (PEG_WATCH*100) + '% ELEV>' + (PEG_ELEVATED*100) + '% CRIT>' + (PEG_CRITICAL*100) + '%');
console.log('    De-escalation:    ' + DEESC_N + ' sustained good epochs');
console.log('    Trend window:     ' + TREND_WIN + ' consecutive declining epochs');
// PRE-REQUEST: Build dynamic contract payload for this epoch
// ── Optional: New T-bill purchase on Day 8+ if cash > $25K ──
id: 'tbill-4wk-new', type: 'tbill', face: purchaseAmt, hqla: 'L1', quality: 100,
description: 'NEW 4-week T-Bill ($' + purchaseAmt + ') — purchased epoch ' + epochNum + ' from fee income'
console.log('NEW T-BILL PURCHASED: $' + purchaseAmt + ' at ' + currentISO);
var body = JSON.stringify({ contracts: contracts, riskFactors: [] }, null, 2);
// Computes all 11 behavioral models, determines mint status,
// decides next cadence adaptively, loops or terminates
// ── Interpolate risk factors at current sim hour ──
// ── Parse ACTUS response → extract notional per contract ──
// Build lookup from portfolio for duration/quality
// AssetQualityModel: degrade quality based on stress
// DeFi exploit at hour 84-108 (Day 3.5-4.5): quality drops to 35
totalFace += notional; totalMtM += mtm; weightedQuality += mtm * quality;
var d = (new Date(c.maturityDate).getTime() - new Date('2026-03-03T00:00:00Z').getTime() - simHour*3600000) / 86400000;
var daysToMat = Math.max(0, (new Date(c.maturityDate).getTime() - new Date('2026-03-03T00:00:00Z').getTime() - simHour*3600000) / 86400000);
var backingDeclining = true, liqDeclining = true, qualDeclining = true;
// ADAPTIVE CADENCE DECISION (truly computed, both directions)
} else if (ewSignals >= 1 || pegDeviation >= CFG.pegElevated || nearThreshold || trendSignal === 'TREND_DEGRADING') {
// Allow de-escalation but only ONE level at a time
console.log('  ↓ DE-ESCALATED: ' + prevCadence + ' → ' + newCadence + ' (after ' + CFG.deescN + ' good epochs)');
// Hold current cadence until streak is met
console.log('  ⏳ De-escalation pending: ' + goodStreak + '/' + CFG.deescN + ' good epochs');
// Conditions not met — reset streak, stay at current
var intervalMap = {NORMAL: CFG.normalHrs, WATCH: CFG.watchHrs, ELEVATED: CFG.elevatedHrs, CRITICAL: CFG.criticalHrs};
// ── Update portfolio: drain cash from redemptions ──
var drain = Math.min(cashC.face * 0.9, hourlyDrain); // Cap at 90% to avoid negative
// Also reduce token supply from redemptions
epoch: epochNum, hour: simHour, datetime: epochDatetime,
cadence: newCadence, interval: nextInterval, prevCadence: prevCadence,
contracts: contractCount, totalFace: totalFace, totalMtM: totalMtM,
backingMtM: backingMtM, liquidity: liquidityRatio, quality: avgQuality,
hhi: hhi, wam: wam, ewSignals: ewSignals, ewPosture: ewPosture,
console.log('║  EPOCH ' + String(epochNum).padStart(3) + ' │ ' + epochDatetime + ' │ Hour ' + simHour.toFixed(0).padStart(4) + ' │ ' + cadenceIcon + ' ' + newCadence.padEnd(8) + '  ║');
console.log('║  Contracts: ' + contractCount + ' │ Face: $' + totalFace.toLocaleString().padStart(8) + ' │ MtM: $' + totalMtM.toFixed(0).padStart(8) + '       ║');
console.log('║  🏦 MINT: ' + mintStatus.padEnd(8) + mintIcon + '  │ Next check in ' + nextInterval + 'h (' + newCadence + ')              ║');
console.log('\n⏹  SIMULATION END: reached ' + CFG.simEndHours + 'h limit (' + (CFG.simEndHours/24).toFixed(0) + ' days)');
postman.setNextRequest('═══ FINAL SUMMARY: Adaptive Timeline Dashboard ═══');
console.log('\n⏹  SIMULATION END: reached MAX_EPOCHS cap (' + CFG.maxEpochs + ')');
postman.setNextRequest('═══ FINAL SUMMARY: Adaptive Timeline Dashboard ═══');
pm.test('Epoch ' + epochNum + ': ACTUS returned contracts', function(){ pm.expect(contractCount).to.be.above(0); });
console.log('║  APPROACH 3 iter5: ADAPTIVE EPOCH MONITORING — FULL TIMELINE                                 ║');
console.log('║  Epochs: ' + history.length + ' │ Escalations: ' + escCount + ' │ Adaptive cadence (NORMAL/WATCH/ELEVATED/CRITICAL)              ║');
transitions.push({epoch: history[i].epoch, hour: history[i].hour, from: history[i-1].mintStatus, to: history[i].mintStatus});
console.log('║    No transitions (mint status stable throughout)                                            ║');
console.log('║    Epoch ' + t.epoch + ' (Hour ' + t.hour.toFixed(0) + '): ' + t.from + ' → ' + t.to + '                                                   ║');
console.log('║  KEY INSIGHT: Monitoring cadence adapts to risk state, NOT pre-planned.                      ║');
console.log('║  The same collection with different risk factor curves produces different cadence patterns.   ║');
console.log('║  In production: CRE cron interval = current cadence. Configured via collection variables.    ║');
console.log('║  CONFIGURABLE BOUNDS (override via collection variables):                                    ║');
console.log('║    MAX_EPOCHS, SIM_END_HOURS, *_HRS intervals, *_THRESHOLD, PEG_*, DEESC_SUSTAINED          ║');
pm.test('Timeline complete: ' + history.length + ' epochs', function(){ pm.expect(history.length).to.be.above(0); });
pm.test('At least one cadence escalation occurred', function(){ pm.expect(parseInt(escCount)).to.be.above(0); });
